title: myroxylon seemannii
intro: Myroxylon seemannii, also known as Palo Santo, is a tropical tree native to South America, specifically found in Ecuador, Peru, and the Galapagos Islands. The tree's resin has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Palo Santo include reducing stress and anxiety, relieving pain and inflammation, and promoting relaxation and better sleep. Herbal preparations of Palo Santo include essential oil, incense sticks, and wood chips, which can be used in aromatherapy, meditation, and other holistic practices.
